title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. racemulosa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. racemulosa, also known as the lady's mantle or bear's foot, is a perennial herb belonging to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. racemulosa include soothing and calming effects on the digestive system and skin, as well as its ability to help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as gastrointestinal problems and skin irritations.
